interval_description: Initial series every 2-4 weeks between 6 and 16 weeks of age; final dose at or after 16 weeks. Booster 1 year later, then every 3 years.
notes: Distemper, Adenovirus (hepatitis), Parvovirus, Parainfluenza. Core vaccine for all dogs per AAHA.

interval_description: Single dose at 12-16 weeks of age; booster 1 year later; then every 1 or 3 years depending on product label and local law.
notes: Core vaccine. Legally required in most US/Canada jurisdictions.

interval_description: Initial dose at 12 weeks or older, second dose 2-4 weeks later, then annually.
notes: Reclassified as core by AAHA 2022 due to widespread exposure risk in the US/Canada.

interval_description: Recommended for dogs with boarding, daycare, grooming, or group exposure. Intranasal or oral: single dose; injectable: two doses 2-4 weeks apart. Booster annually (or every 6 months for high-risk dogs).
notes: Non-core (lifestyle) vaccine. Protects against kennel cough.

interval_description: Two initial doses 2-4 weeks apart starting at 12 weeks; booster annually. Recommended for dogs in tick-endemic regions (northeast US, upper midwest, parts of Canada).
notes: Non-core (lifestyle) vaccine. Combine with year-round tick prevention.

interval_description: Initial series every 3-4 weeks from 6-8 weeks through at least 16 weeks of age. Booster 1 year later, then every 3 years for low-risk adults.
notes: Feline Viral Rhinotracheitis, Calicivirus, Panleukopenia. Core vaccine for all cats per AAFP.

interval_description: Single dose at 12-16 weeks of age; booster 1 year later; then per product label (1 or 3 years) and local law.
notes: Core vaccine. Legally required in most US/Canada jurisdictions.

interval_description: Two doses 3-4 weeks apart starting at 8 weeks of age. Considered core for all kittens. Adults: annually for at-risk cats (outdoor/multi-cat), may be discontinued for strictly indoor, single-cat households.
notes: Feline Leukemia Virus. AAFP recommends FeLV testing before vaccination.
